commands.review_summary:
The branch wraps plugin `registerTool` factories and returned `prepareArguments`/`execute` callbacks in the registering plugin's runtime scope and adds focused plugin-thread-isolation regression tests.

PR surface: Source +94, Tests +294. Total +388 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection rather than a live run: current main stores raw plugin tool factories and the agent loop calls `prepareArguments`/`execute` under the ambient context. The added PR tests encode the expected owning-plugin scope path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer review is needed because this protected maintainer PR changes plugin callback identity and security-boundary semantics rather than leaving a narrow repairable defect.

target.title: fix(plugins): scope plugin tool callbacks
target.body:
## Summary

This PR scopes plugin-owned agent tool callbacks to the registering plugin runtime scope.

- Adds `src/plugins/plugin-callback-scope.ts` and wraps `api.registerTool(...)` registrations in `src/plugins/registry.ts`.
- Scopes plugin tool factories, `prepareArguments`, and `execute` callbacks with the registering plugin id/source.
- Covers function factories, static tool objects, array-returned tools, concurrent execution, and error restoration paths.
- Deliberately does not add worker/process sandboxing, conversation/session isolation, SDK-global wrapping, or test isolation.

Review focus: `src/plugins/plugin-callback-scope.ts` should keep the ownership boundary tight without adding request-time discovery or changing non-plugin runtime behavior.

## Linked context

Maintainer requested plugin-focused thread isolation only. Things behind the SDK should stay normal.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: plugin-owned tool factories, `prepareArguments`, and `execute` callbacks now run under the owning plugin runtime scope instead of inheriting only the caller's ambient gateway scope.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout at PR head, plus a temporary full local worktree at the same PR stack for non-sparse typecheck/lint/build proof. Testbox-through-Crabbox was also attempted; see proof limitations.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/plugins/plugin-tool-thread-isolation.e2e.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/runtime/gateway-request-scope.test.ts src/plugins/runtime/runtime-config.test.ts src/plugins/registry.runtime-config.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs
node scripts/check-changelog-attributions.mjs
node scripts/check-extension-wildcard-reexports.mjs
node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-wildcard-reexports.mjs
node scripts/check-duplicates.mjs --coverage
node scripts/check-dependency-pins.mjs
node scripts/check-package-patches.mjs
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --split-core
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs
```

Evidence after fix: focused plugin e2e passed with 3 tests; adjacent runtime scope/config tests passed with 10 tests; final autoreview returned clean with no accepted/actionable findings; the full-worktree changed-check-equivalent guard/typecheck/lint sequence passed; build-all passed with the no-pnpm fallback.

Observed result after fix: plugin factories, `prepareArguments`, and `execute` callbacks observed their owning plugin ids/sources; the outer gateway scope was restored after success and after a thrown prepare callback; array factory results were all wrapped.

What was not tested: live installed third-party plugins, worker/process sandboxing, conversation/session routing isolation, or test-runner isolation. Those are intentionally out of scope.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: direct `pnpm check:changed` is blocked in Codex/sparse worktrees by pnpm dependency reconciliation. Testbox-through-Crabbox first caught a real branch lint issue, then subsequent reruns stalled after remote dependency hydration before script execution; those current-task boxes were stopped. The equivalent changed-check commands were run directly in a temporary full worktree instead, and a final focused e2e sanity pass was run after rebasing onto the latest `origin/main` before push.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `No`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: plugin tool callback wrapping.

Risk mitigation: the wrapper only applies to plugin `api.registerTool(...)` callbacks, uses the existing plugin runtime `AsyncLocalStorage` scope helper, preserves the original tool surface, and does not introduce loader workers, conversation/session routing, or new discovery/cache paths.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

Still waiting on: PR CI from the latest push.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: local autoreview finding for unscoped `prepareArguments` was fixed and final autoreview is clean.
